channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Iowa's top leaders are strongly endorsing Musk and DOGE. Iowans are watching closely
item.description: Iowa Sen. Joni Ernst has hailed Elon Musk and the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump meets with congressional Republicans as GOP lawmakers argue over tax and spending cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump hosted an unusually long meeting with House Republicans at the White House on Thursday, turning over prime workspace for them to hammer out differences over the size, scope and details of their multitrillion-dollar plan to cut taxes, regulations and governmen
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis backs Trump’s plans for FEMA, DOGE efforts
item.description: The governor announced his support for President Donald Trump looking to remove FEMA as a middleman after a disaster.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker pushes bold legislation to protect homeownership from corporations
item.description: In what's being called a "bold new measure," one Tampa Bay lawmaker wants to put families over corporate elites by making sure local Floridians can stay in their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Out of power: Democrats disoriented in fight against Trump agenda
item.description: A new Congress and a new presidential administration places both parties back at square one – and Republicans have an abundantly clear upper hand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Member of Elon Musk’s DOGE team resigns after racist posts resurface
item.description: Marko Elez had recently been given special access by a federal judge to highly sensitive payment systems in the Department of Treasury.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Federal workers need more time on buyout decision: Money expert
item.description: Millions of U.S. government employees must make a decision whether to accept a buyout offer from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: Blur of action in Washington, back to action in St. Paul
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News host Brian Bakst talks about the blistering change in Washington with U.S. Sen. Tina Smith. And the scuffle in the Minnesota House is over. We’ll discuss how lawmakers struck a deal to gavel in the session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Chris Swanson, prominent sheriff and Democrat, announces run for Michigan governor
item.description: Genesee County Chris Swanson has told The Associated Press he's launching a Democratic bid to be Michigan's next governor.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge pauses Trump federal worker buyouts
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday extended the deadline for federal workers to accept the Trump administration’s sweeping buyout offer. The court will weigh the merits of the bid to block the offer in a Monday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats delay Kash Patel committee vote
item.description: Voting on Patel's nomination was on the agenda for Judiciary members, but Democrats requested it be held over one week.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Ben Stiller denies being paid by USAID to go to Ukraine
item.description: The reportedly debunked social media post was shared by billionaire Elon Musk, who owns X, and Donald Trump Jr..
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: CIA shares new employee names through unclassified email
item.description: The nature of the communication risks compromising the identifies of the agents — sparking condemnation by leaders.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Israel defense minister tells army to prepare to relocate Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: He says the military should prepare exit options by land, sea and air so that anyone who wishes to can leave for "any country willing to accept them.
item.dc:creator: Jerome Socolovsky and Ryland Barton, MPR News

item.title: FBI agents, prosecutors fear retribution from Jan. 6 rioters pardoned by Trump
item.description: Officials involved in Jan. 6 prosecutions say the Trump administration isn't protecting them from threats. "We don't think they'll care — unless and until one of us gets killed," an official told NPR.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Department of Education: The arguments for, against killing it
item.description: Former Trump campaign surrogate Scotti Nell Hughes says the move would bring more accountability at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: New attorney general moves to align Justice Department with Trump's priorities
item.description: Trump and Attorney General Pam Bondi have both argued the Justice Department under Biden unfairly targeted conservatives, most notably Trump himself.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: 60 Minutes publishes transcript of Harris interview amid FCC investigation
item.description: "We are posting the same transcripts and videos of our interview with Vice President Kamala Harris that we provided to the FCC," the news program wrote on its website.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic attorneys general pledge to protect gender-affirming care following Trump order
item.description: More than a dozen Democratic state attorneys general on Wednesday affirmed their support for gender-affirming health care for transgender youth after an executive order issued late last week by President Trump threatened federal support for treatments such as puberty blockers, hormones and surgery f
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: White House defends Trump's Gaza takeover, resettlement proposal
item.description: The White House on Wednesday defended President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take control of the Gaza Strip amid blowback from Arab leaders and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats accuse DOGE of going after NOAA
item.description: Democratic lawmakers on Wednesday accused Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) of improperly inserting itself into the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). In a joint statement, Reps. Zoe Lofgren (D-Calif.) and Jared Huffman (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Walz sets special election for seat in suburban Ramsey County that will decide House power
item.description: A special election will fill a seat won by a Democrat in the November election that came open after a residency challenge. The result could return the Minnesota House to a tie or boost a GOP majority.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Turner to lead HUD
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Scott Turner to lead the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), cementing another one of President Trump’s Cabinet choices. Senators voted 55-44 on Turner’s nomination. Sens. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) and Peter Welch (D-Vt.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada prisons director points to 4-mile fence, better staffing; inmate food issues persist
item.description: A high-profile inmate escape, the resignation of the prisons director, and reports that fences, doors and windows were vulnerable. It all added up to a spotlight on the Nevada Department of Corrections in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spent some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Senate was a battleground in 2023, new Republican minority leader emphasizes setting priorities
item.description: The Nevada Senate is ready to get down to business, and the end of the 2023 Legislature is still fresh in the minds of Democrats and Republicans gathered in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Legislature observes 'family day,' targets high price of eggs as session starts
item.description: State lawmakers convened in Carson City on Monday, beginning the 83rd Legislative Session with the legislative chambers filled with family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC mayor downplays economic concerns of Trump deportation plan
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There have anecdotal reports that immigration raids are driving undocumented New Yorkers into the shadows. New York City has more than 400,000 undocumented workers who help prop up the economy; however, Mayor Eric Adams said he was not worried they would stop working.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: President Donald Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration's push for a federal funding freeze is back in court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A planned Trump administration freeze on federal funding is heading back to a Washington courtroom on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC
